repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

LSP spec allows response message to have a `null` request id ( https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#responseMessage ).
This may happen when for example server cannot identify request id due to unparsable message from Neovim LSP client.

The previous implementation only checked if `response.id` field is lua-`null`, not `vim.NIL`.
As vim.NIL (the Lua representation of JSON null) is truthy,
so these responses were incorrectly dispatched as normal responses,
causing errors in vim._assert_integer or silent failures.

## Solution

Guard the server response branches against id == vim.NIL,
and add an explicit branch to handle error responses with null id
by logging a warning and dispatching on error.

comment_data.body:
I think the client shouldn't gracefully handle invalid server requests that violate the spec. Yea, the code will fail somewhere down the call stack upon encountering an invalid value, but there are numerous potential cases beyond just `null`. And also you can think of validating request and response bodies and so on, but it is the price of speed on the hot code path in exchange for less informative error messages. But since the server is broken anyway, it doesn't really matter for the end user.
Reflecting on my similar change in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/35264 I completely agree with @MariaSolOs that it is the right approach.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -477,6 +478,15 @@ function Client:handle_body(body)
       self:on_error(M.client_errors.NO_RESULT_CALLBACK_FOUND, decoded)
       log.error('No callback found for server response id ' .. result_id)
     end
+  elseif decoded.id == vim.NIL and decoded.error ~= nil and decoded.error ~= vim.NIL then
+    log.warn('Server sent error response with null id', decoded.error)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)
+  elseif type(decoded.method) == 'string' and decoded.id == vim.NIL then
+    -- Server request with null id is invalid per JSON-RPC 2.0 (id SHOULD NOT be null).
+    -- Do not fall through to the notification branch: the presence of id (even null)
+    -- distinguishes a request from a notification (§4.1).
+    log.warn('Server sent request with null id', decoded.method)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)

comment_data.body:
Is `decoded.error ~= vim.NIL` check needed here? The spec does not state that it is allowed value:
```
/**
 * The error object in case a request fails.
 */
error?: ResponseError;
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:

       log.error('No callback found for server response id ' .. result_id)
     end
+  elseif decoded.id == vim.NIL and decoded.error ~= nil and decoded.error ~= vim.NIL then

comment_data.body:
correct me if wrong, but this change does not look it is attempting to "heal" an invalid state. It looks like this is logging and failing early(?).

If we can fail earlier then that is better for us and users. Otherwise bugs are just harder to troubleshoot.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -477,6 +478,15 @@ function Client:handle_body(body)
       self:on_error(M.client_errors.NO_RESULT_CALLBACK_FOUND, decoded)
       log.error('No callback found for server response id ' .. result_id)

+  elseif decoded.id == vim.NIL and decoded.error ~= nil and decoded.error ~= vim.NIL then
+    log.warn('Server sent error response with null id', decoded.error)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)
+  elseif type(decoded.method) == 'string' and decoded.id == vim.NIL then
+    -- Server request with null id is invalid per JSON-RPC 2.0 (id SHOULD NOT be null).
+    -- Do not fall through to the notification branch: the presence of id (even null)

+    log.warn('Server sent request with null id', decoded.method)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)

comment_data.body:
> correct me if wrong, but this change does not look it is attempting to "heal" an invalid state. It looks like this is logging and failing early(?).
> 
> If we can fail earlier then that is better for us and users. Otherwise bugs are just harder to troubleshoot.

By "gracefully" handling I meant that it checks and logs the specific incorrect state, which would fail anyway. The problem here is that this state is not allowed by the spec and is therefore completetely arbitrary. This check addresses cases where a request has ID `null`. But what if the other day, a server author mistakenly placed a boolean there? While that's just as out of spec as null, client shouldn't need to account for all such cases.
Perhaps this check could be properly generalized to assert for either a string or a number, but I question what additional value that would provide beyond a slight hint toward the bug in server's JSONRPC implementation.

comment_data.body:
@skewb1k thanks for the comment. should we apply generalized validation like below?

```diff
- elseif type(decoded.method) == 'string' and decoded.id == vim.NIL then
+ elseif type(decoded.method) == 'string' and type(decoded.id) ~= "number" and type(decoded.id) ~= "string" then
```

comment_data.body:
> The problem here is that this state is not allowed by the spec and is therefore completetely arbitrary. This check addresses cases where a request has ID `null`. But what if the other day, a server author mistakenly placed a boolean there? While that's just as out of spec as null, client shouldn't need to account for all such cases.

That's not really a problem. Incomplete fail-early checks are a net gain, even if they are incomplete. And sure, generalizing the check is a TODO that we can consider in the future. Maybe add a comment here mentioning that.

comment_data.body:
I think its clearer to move this assertion to the first method call handling branch on L384:
```lua
assert(
  type(decoded.id) == 'number' or type(decoded.id) == 'string',
  'Request id must be a number or a string'
)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -477,6 +478,15 @@ function Client:handle_body(body)
       self:on_error(M.client_errors.NO_RESULT_CALLBACK_FOUND, decoded)
       log.error('No callback found for server response id ' .. result_id)
     end
+  elseif decoded.id == vim.NIL then
+    log.warn('Server sent response with null id', decoded.error)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)
+  elseif
+    type(decoded.method) == 'string'
+    and not vim.tbl_contains({ 'number', 'string' }, type(decoded.id))
+  then
+    log.warn('Server sent request with invalid id', decoded.method, decoded.id)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)

@@ -477,6 +478,15 @@ function Client:handle_body(body)
       self:on_error(M.client_errors.NO_RESULT_CALLBACK_FOUND, decoded)
       log.error('No callback found for server response id ' .. result_id)
     end
+  elseif decoded.id == vim.NIL then
+    log.warn('Server sent response with null id', decoded.error)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)
+  elseif
+    type(decoded.method) == 'string'
+    and not vim.tbl_contains({ 'number', 'string' }, type(decoded.id))
+  then
+    log.warn('Server sent request with invalid id', decoded.method, decoded.id)
+    self:on_error(M.client_errors.INVALID_SERVER_MESSAGE, decoded)

comment_regular_data.body:
@skewb1k 

> I'm curious about how the server can determine that it received a request if it cannot extract the ID to send back? 

To answer your question, I found null-request-id response during didChange notification.
[tower-lsp-server](https://github.com/tower-lsp-community/tower-lsp-server) received  unparsable message due to the bug I am trying to solve in the following PR.
Due to the parse error of the whole message, tower-lsp-server cannot determine the request id of the message.

- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38341
Will plan to merge this early next week, after 0.12 release

title: fix(lsp): handle nullable id in JSON-RPC responses per spec
